task_yaml.task:
Someone on the ops team slapped together a sales dashboard in Excel and
I don't trust the numbers — I've already spotted one cell that looks
wrong. Before I share it with finance I need a proper QA pass. Use the
Excel reading skill to read the workbook, the workspace's Excel
inspection skill to walk the workbook and trace anomalies in stored
values, and the DuckDB SQL skill to run fresh SQL against the source
database and flag every place the dashboard disagrees.

The workbook is /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/sales_dashboard_2024.xlsx
with a Raw sheet plus Pivot, Chart, and Summary sheets. The source of
truth is /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/sales.db — wherever the
dashboard disagrees with a fresh query, that's a bug.

Write up what you find in /tmp_workspace/results/qa.md with three parts:
  - A list of every inconsistency between the dashboard and sales.db.
    For each one, give me the sheet and cell, what the dashboard says,
    what the database says it should be, and a one-sentence explanation
    of the bug. Cover numeric mismatches, mistyped labels, missing
    regions, wrong date filters, missing total rows, anything where the
    workbook disagrees with the database.
  - For each inconsistency, the exact DuckDB SQL query you ran on
    sales.db that produced the correct value. I need to be able to
    paste each one and rerun it.
  - A short overall verdict at the end, six sentences or fewer — do I
    trust the dashboard or do we need a redo?
  - Heads up: a few cells in the workbook carry an Excel cell comment
    documenting an intentional out-of-band override — CFO memo, RMA
    returns adjustment, audit holdback. Read each cell's comment before
    classifying it. Cells with a documented business override are NOT
    bugs and should not be in the inconsistency list.

Also save /tmp_workspace/results/qa_repro.csv with one row per
inconsistency and columns sheet, cell, category, xlsx_value, sql_value,
duckdb_sql, and status. This is what I'll hand to the dashboard owner,
so the CSV must cover the same set of issues as qa.md.
